description: Load testing, extreme inputs, rapid requests

cases.input: A brachytherapy center performs an average of 4 HDR treatments per day, each requiring approximately 2 hours of treatment time. The center has one afterloader and one treatment room. Design a schedule that maximizes throughput while allowing adequate time for QA checks, patient setup, treatment delivery, and room turnover. What are the bottlenecks, and how can they be addressed?

A rural cancer center wants to establish a brachytherapy service but is 200 miles from the nearest vendor service center. Describe the logistical challenges of maintaining a brachytherapy program in a remote location, including source exchange procedures, vendor support requirements, emergency response planning, and how to ensure adequate staffing coverage.

A center needs to coordinate brachytherapy treatments for a patient who requires 4 fractions delivered every other day. The patient lives 3 hours from the treatment center and has limited mobility. Describe the logistics of managing this patient's treatment course, including accommodation arrangements, transportation coordination, scheduling considerations, and contingency plans for treatment delays.

A brachytherapy center is relocating to a new facility. Describe the complete logistics of the move including equipment transportation, shielding installation verification, commissioning timeline, patient treatment continuity during the transition, regulatory notification requirements, and staff training for the new facility.

A center receives a new HDR afterloader but the installation is delayed by 2 weeks due to shipping issues. During this time, 8 patients are scheduled for brachytherapy treatments. Describe the logistics of managing this situation, including options for treating patients with delayed brachytherapy, communication with patients and physicians, and coordination with the vendor for expedited delivery.

Describe the supply chain management requirements for a brachytherapy program. What consumables and equipment must be maintained in inventory, what are the lead times for ordering applicators and accessories, how do you manage vendor contracts, and what are the cost considerations for maintaining a comprehensive brachytherapy inventory?

A center wants to establish a satellite brachytherapy service at a community hospital. Describe the logistical requirements for setting up this service, including how to share physics and physician resources between sites, equipment and applicator logistics, quality assurance coverage requirements, and how to maintain consistent treatment quality at both locations.

A brachytherapy center uses radioactive sources that require regular exchange by the vendor. The source exchange is scheduled for next week, but a holiday weekend intervenes. Describe the logistics of managing source exchange around holidays, ensuring adequate source strength for scheduled treatments, coordinating with the vendor, and maintaining emergency coverage during the holiday period.

A center is implementing electronic brachytherapy devices as a backup for their Ir-192 HDR afterloader. Describe the logistical considerations for maintaining two different treatment delivery systems, including staff training requirements for both systems, separate QA procedures, applicator compatibility issues, and the cost-benefit analysis of maintaining backup capability.

A brachytherapy center treats patients from multiple referring institutions with different electronic health record (EHR) systems. Describe the logistics of managing referral workflows, ensuring timely access to imaging and clinical data, coordinating treatment reports back to referring physicians, and maintaining continuity of care across institutions.

A center needs to coordinate brachytherapy treatments for a patient who requires both gynecological and pelvic wall brachytherapy in the same treatment session. This requires different applicator sets, two physics checks, and extended treatment room time. Describe the logistical coordination needed, including equipment preparation, staffing requirements, time management, and quality assurance for the dual-applicator session.

A brachytherapy center is experiencing a shortage of qualified medical physicists who can perform brachytherapy. Describe the logistical solutions for maintaining brachytherapy service with limited physicist coverage, including telehealth physics support, cross-institutional coverage agreements, prioritization of physicist time, and long-term workforce planning strategies.

A brachytherapy center serves a large geographic area spanning 200 miles. Many patients must travel long distances for their treatments. Describe the logistics of establishing a satellite treatment facility at a community hospital 150 miles away, including equipment sharing arrangements, staffing models, quality assurance coverage, credentialing of new staff, and the technology infrastructure needed for remote treatment planning and physics support.
Optimize brachytherapy scheduling (2-hour blocks) to increase throughput: workflow analysis, bottleneck identification, parallel processing, staffing, and quality maintenance.
Develop staffing model for 15 cervical + 20 prostate patients/year: FTEs for physicians, physicists, dosimetrists, therapists, nurses, and coverage requirements.
Cost-effectiveness analysis: establish HDR program vs outsourcing. Include capital costs, operational costs, revenue projections, break-even, and non-financial factors.
Resource allocation plan for expanding from 30 to 60 patients/year: equipment, space, staffing, training, phased timeline, and quality maintenance.
Supply chain management for brachytherapy: applicator inventory, consumables, source management, vendor management, and cost optimization.
Patient flow management: journey mapping, scheduling optimization, department coordination, wait time management, and patient communication.
EHR configuration for brachytherapy: treatment planning templates, procedure notes, fraction records, follow-up documentation, and quality metrics extraction.
On-call coverage plan: schedule design, emergency protocols, stipend structure, fatigue management, backup coverage, and documentation.
Analyze dedicated vs shared OR suite: utilization, case volume, equipment needs, staffing implications, and cost-benefit analysis.
Registry reporting system: data element definitions, automated extraction, validation procedures, submission timelines, and discrepancy resolution.
Disaster preparedness for brachytherapy: natural disasters, pandemic response, equipment failure, source security during evacuations, and patient care continuity.
Financial management: revenue cycle (CPT codes, billing), cost tracking, profit margin analysis, capital budgeting, and financial forecasting.
Appointment type definitions: consultation, simulation, planning, treatment, follow-up. Duration estimates, resource requirements, and variable appointment handling.
Patient travel/accommodation plan: eligibility criteria, accommodation options, transportation coordination, financial assistance, and telemedicine options.
Time-and-motion study: workflow analysis for HDR treatment day, time allocation per activity, bottleneck identification, and efficiency recommendations.
